title: B1 Intermediate English
description: Intermediate-level vocabulary for learners who can handle everyday topics and common academic or professional situations.

cards.meaning: to successfully reach a goal after effort
cards.example: She worked hard to achieve her dream.

cards.meaning: to have an influence on something or someone
cards.example: Stress can affect your health.

cards.meaning: to permit someone to do something
cards.example: They do not allow smoking inside the building.

cards.meaning: to come into sight or seem to be
cards.example: She appeared calm despite the pressure.

cards.meaning: to make a formal request or to put something into use
cards.example: I will apply for that job tomorrow.

cards.meaning: to come near to something or a way of dealing with something
cards.example: We need a new approach to solve this problem.

cards.meaning: to plan or organise something
cards.example: She arranged a meeting for Friday.

cards.meaning: to stay away from or prevent something
cards.example: Try to avoid eating too much sugar.

cards.meaning: to start to be something
cards.example: He wants to become a doctor.

cards.meaning: to think that something is true
cards.example: I believe she is telling the truth.

cards.meaning: to be the property of or a member of
cards.example: This book belongs to me.

cards.meaning: an advantage or positive result
cards.example: Exercise has many health benefits.

cards.meaning: to make something happen
cards.example: Smoking can cause serious illness.

cards.meaning: to become or make different
cards.example: The weather can change quickly here.

cards.meaning: to select one option from several
cards.example: You can choose any book you like.

cards.meaning: to look at similarities and differences
cards.example: Let's compare the two plans carefully.

cards.meaning: to finish something
cards.example: Please complete the form and return it.

cards.meaning: to be about something; worry or interest
cards.example: This issue concerns everyone in the team.

cards.meaning: to think carefully about something
cards.example: Please consider all your options before deciding.

cards.meaning: to keep doing something without stopping
cards.example: She continued to study even when she was tired.

cards.meaning: to manage or direct something
cards.example: He could not control his anger.

cards.meaning: to make or produce something new
cards.example: She created a beautiful painting.

cards.meaning: to handle a situation or do business
cards.example: We need to deal with this problem now.

cards.meaning: to make a choice after thinking
cards.example: I decided to study abroad.

cards.meaning: to explain the meaning of something
cards.example: Can you define the word 'democracy'?

cards.meaning: to give details about something
cards.example: Describe what you saw at the scene.

cards.meaning: to grow or improve over time
cards.example: They are developing a new app.

cards.meaning: to be unlike something else
cards.example: Our opinions differ on this topic.

cards.meaning: to talk about something with others
cards.example: Let's discuss the plan after lunch.

cards.meaning: to separate into parts
cards.example: Divide the pizza equally among the four of us.

cards.meaning: a result or change caused by something
cards.example: The medicine had a positive effect on her.

cards.meaning: to make it possible for something to happen
cards.example: Technology enables us to work from home.

cards.meaning: to give someone support or confidence
cards.example: My parents encouraged me to follow my dreams.

cards.meaning: to set up or create something formally
cards.example: They established a new school in the village.

cards.meaning: to be real or live
cards.example: Do you believe that aliens exist?

cards.meaning: to make something clear and easy to understand
cards.example: Can you explain how this machine works?

cards.meaning: to show a feeling or idea clearly
cards.example: It is important to express your feelings.

cards.meaning: to not succeed in something
cards.example: He failed the exam and felt disappointed.

cards.meaning: to have an emotion or physical sensation
cards.example: I feel tired after working all day.

cards.meaning: to concentrate on one thing
cards.example: Focus on what you can control.

cards.meaning: to go after someone or to obey instructions
cards.example: Follow the instructions carefully.

cards.meaning: to take place or occur
cards.example: What happened at the meeting?

cards.meaning: to recognise or name something
cards.example: Can you identify the problem?

cards.meaning: to make or become better
cards.example: She improved her English by reading every day.

cards.meaning: to have something as part of a whole
cards.example: The price includes breakfast.

cards.meaning: to become or make larger in number or amount
cards.example: Prices have increased a lot this year.

cards.meaning: the power to affect someone or something
cards.example: Her mother had a great influence on her career.

cards.meaning: to become a member of a group or connect things
cards.example: I want to join the football club.

cards.meaning: to continue to have or do something
cards.example: Keep practising and you will improve.

cards.meaning: to guide or be in charge of others
cards.example: She leads a team of ten people.

cards.meaning: to gain knowledge or a skill
cards.example: I am learning to play the guitar.

cards.meaning: to control or succeed in doing something difficult
cards.example: He manages a large team at work.

cards.meaning: to find the size or amount of something
cards.example: Measure the room before buying the carpet.

cards.meaning: to refer to something briefly
cards.example: She mentioned that she was moving abroad.

cards.meaning: to go from one place to another
cards.example: We plan to move to a bigger flat next year.

cards.meaning: to present something for acceptance
cards.example: He offered to help with the project.

cards.meaning: to arrange things in an orderly way
cards.example: She organized the files on her computer.

cards.meaning: to carry out a task or present a show
cards.example: The students performed a play at school.

cards.meaning: to decide in advance what to do
cards.example: We need to plan the trip carefully.

cards.meaning: to make ready for something
cards.example: I prepared dinner for the whole family.

cards.meaning: to stop something from happening
cards.example: Wash your hands to prevent illness.

cards.meaning: to make or create something
cards.example: This factory produces cars.

cards.meaning: to show that something is true
cards.example: He proved that he was innocent.

cards.meaning: to give or supply something
cards.example: The company provides free meals for employees.

cards.meaning: to arrive at a place or achieve a goal
cards.example: We reached the top of the mountain at noon.

cards.meaning: to become aware of something
cards.example: I suddenly realized I had forgotten my keys.

cards.meaning: to get or accept something
cards.example: She received a letter from her friend.

cards.meaning: to make smaller in amount or size
cards.example: We should reduce our use of plastic.

cards.meaning: to mention or direct someone to a source
cards.example: Please refer to page ten for more information.

cards.meaning: to connect or have a relationship with
cards.example: How does this topic relate to what we studied?

cards.meaning: to stay in a place or continue to be
cards.example: Please remain seated during the flight.

cards.meaning: to keep information in your mind
cards.example: Do you remember our first meeting?

cards.meaning: to take something away from a place
cards.example: Remove your shoes before entering the house.

cards.meaning: to put something new in the place of another
cards.example: We need to replace the old computer.

cards.meaning: to give information about something officially
cards.example: He reported the accident to the police.

cards.meaning: to say or act in reply to something
cards.example: Please respond to my email as soon as possible.

cards.meaning: the outcome or consequence of something
cards.example: Hard work always results in success.

cards.meaning: to come or go back
cards.example: I will return the book to the library tomorrow.

cards.meaning: to look carefully for something
cards.example: He searched everywhere for his keys.

cards.meaning: to appear to be something
cards.example: She seems unhappy today.

cards.meaning: to provide a service or food to someone
cards.example: The waiter served the food quickly.

cards.meaning: to let someone see something
cards.example: Show me your work when you're done.

cards.meaning: to find an answer to a problem
cards.example: We need to solve this issue quickly.

cards.meaning: to use time or money on something
cards.example: I spend two hours studying every day.

cards.meaning: to propose an idea for consideration
cards.example: She suggested going to the park.

cards.meaning: to help or encourage someone
cards.example: My family always supports me.

cards.meaning: to be likely to do or be something
cards.example: She tends to be very organised.

cards.meaning: to change words into another language
cards.example: Can you translate this sentence into French?

cards.meaning: to know the meaning of something
cards.example: Do you understand the instructions?

cards.meaning: an opinion or the ability to see a place
cards.example: She has a positive view of the future.

cards.meaning: to examine or check something
cards.example: The teacher will test us on vocabulary tomorrow.
